data.title: Strategy’s Michael Saylor in talks with MSCI on potential index exclusion
data.description:
Strategy, the world’s biggest corporate stockpiler of Bitcoin, is engaging with MSCI about a decision that could potentially exclude it from its indices. The company risks exclusion from MSCI indices due to its volatile balance sheet. According to reports, Chairman Michael Saylor is directly engaging MSCI ahead of its January 15, 2026, decision. JP Morgan […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/strategy-saylor-talks-with-msci-index/

data.title: ADP Revises October Employment Figures
data.description: According to PANews, the American company Automatic Data Processing (ADP) has revised its employment figures for October. The number of jobs added has been adjusted to 47,000, up from the previously reported 42,000.

data.title: U.S. November ADP Employment Figures Fall Short of Expectations
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the U.S. ADP employment report for November revealed a decrease of 32,000 jobs, marking the lowest level since March 2023. This figure fell short of market expectations, which had anticipated an increase of 10,000 jobs. The latter half of 2025 is projected to see stagnation in U.S. employment growth, with a declining trend in job numbers. In November, hiring was particularly weak in the manufacturing, professional and business services, information, and construction sectors. Following the release of the ADP report, spot gold experienced a brief rise of approximately $6, currently trading at $4,219.53 per ounce.

data.title: Crypto Market Experiences Significant Liquidations Over 24 Hours
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, Coinglass data reveals that the crypto market saw liquidations totaling $482 million in the past 24 hours. Long positions accounted for $75.8192 million, while short positions reached $406 million. Specifically, Bitcoin long positions were liquidated for $16.636 million, and Bitcoin short positions for $221 million. Ethereum long positions faced liquidations of $14.4346 million, with Ethereum short positions at $83.0568 million.

In the same timeframe, 122,348 individuals globally experienced liquidations. The largest single liquidation occurred on Bybit's BTCUSD, valued at $13 million.

data.title: Open Gas Initiative Launches to Reduce Ethereum Gas Costs
data.description:
According to PANews, ETHGas has officially launched the Open Gas Initiative, offering a zero-code gas subsidy solution to help various protocols cover part of the gas costs for users, aiming to create a smoother, low-friction on-chain interaction experience. The initiative is now live on the Ethereum mainnet and will later expand to other EVM public chains and layer-2 networks, including BNB, Base, and Arbitrum.

The initial lineup for the Open Gas Initiative includes protocols such as EigenLayer, ether.fi, Pendle, and Velvet Capital, which will be the first to integrate the initiative. These protocols will demonstrate the potential to enhance user engagement and retention through gas subsidies combined with community goodwill. Infrastructure partners like GasHawk, Blocknative, Goldsky, and ClickHouse will also participate in future integrations, collaborating on transaction optimization, real-time data, and analytics.

The Open Gas Initiative provides protocols with gas rebate structure design and gas cost estimation, while offering users gas expenditure queries and gas ID generation features. ETHGas aims to drive the industry towards a "Gasless Future."

data.title: Parataxis Holdings to Acquire Sinsiway for Ethereum Treasury Transformation
data.description: According to BlockBeats, New York-based digital asset investment firm Parataxis Holdings has agreed to acquire a controlling stake in South Korean data security company Sinsiway for 40 billion Korean won, approximately 27.3 million dollars. The acquisition aims to transform Sinsiway into an Ethereum treasury company. If Sinsiway shareholders approve the deal by January 2026, the company will be renamed Parataxis ETH, Inc. while retaining its stock exchange listing status. This move will establish it as an Ethereum treasury company in the South Korean market, backed by U.S. institutional capital. Parataxis Holdings' founder and CEO, Edward Chin, is expected to join the board of directors.

data.title: U.S. Labor Market Faces Increased Slowdown in November
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, the U.S. labor market experienced a significant slowdown in November, as reported by Jinshi. The ADP report revealed that private companies laid off 32,000 employees during the month, with small businesses being the hardest hit. The data indicated a sharp deterioration in the employment market compared to October, which saw an upward revision to an increase of 47,000 jobs, falling short of the 40,000 job growth predicted by economists surveyed by Dow Jones. This report serves as the final employment data before the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision in December. Futures traders estimate a nearly 90% chance that the Federal Reserve will lower the key interest rate by another 25 basis points.

data.title: Binance Alpha Airdrops 295 H Tokens to Eligible Users
data.description: Binance announced on X that Binance Alpha's second wave of Humanity Protocol (H) airdrop rewards is live.Users with at least 242 Binance Alpha Points can claim an airdrop of 295 H tokens on a first-come, first-served basis. If the rewards are not fully distributed,  the score threshold will automatically decrease by 5 points every 5 minutes.Claiming the airdrop will consume 15 Binance Alpha Points. Users must confirm their claim on the Alpha Events page within 24 hours; otherwise, it will be deemed that users have given up claiming the airdrop.

data.title: BNB Chain's Real-World Asset Value Doubles to $1.6 Billion
data.description: According to PANews, the value of real-world assets on the BNB Chain has doubled over the past 30 days, reaching $1.6 billion. This significant increase highlights the growing interest and investment in blockchain-based assets. The BNB Chain continues to attract attention as it expands its offerings and capabilities in the digital asset space.

data.title: Anthropic Eyes $300 Billion Valuation Amid IPO Plans
data.description: According to Odaily, artificial intelligence company Anthropic is in discussions for a new round of venture capital funding, with an anticipated valuation of $300 billion. The company has also engaged California law firm Wilson Sonsini for advisory services regarding its planned initial public offering (IPO), targeting a 2026 listing. Analysts suggest that the IPO could provide Anthropic with substantial capital, enabling it to compete with Sam Altman's OpenAI. Research by Deutsche Bank analysts Adrian Cox and Stefan Abrudan indicates some vulnerabilities in OpenAI's operations, noting a slowdown in consumer subscription growth for its ChatGPT service. Anthropic is perceived as having a greater potential for profitability compared to OpenAI.
